title: Oldenlandia hedyotidea
intro: Oldenlandia hedyotidea, also known as "sticky grass" or "blood grass," is a perennial plant belonging to the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia hedyotidea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Oldenlandia hedyotidea is often used to make tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Oldenlandia hedyotidea is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family includes around 600 genera and over 13,000 species of plants. The genus Oldenlandia itself contains around 30 species of plants, most of which are herbaceous annuals or perennials. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Oldenlandia hedyotidea shares characteristics with other plants in this group, such as opposite leaves and small, tubular flowers.
